relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Paul turns to Abraham, forefather according to flesh.
If Abraham’s righteousness came from works, boasting would remain—but not before the Divine.
Scripture says Abraham trusted, and trust was counted as justice.
Wage belongs to debt, not grace.
Trust in the One making righteous the ungodly is counted as righteousness.
David blesses the human counted righteous apart from works.
Blessed are released deeds and covered misalignments.
Blessed is the one whose misalignment is not counted.
The blessedness reaches beyond circumcision.
Abraham was counted righteous while uncircumcised.
Circumcision becomes sign and seal, not the origin of trust.
Abraham fathers those walking in trust’s footsteps.
The world-inheritance promise comes through trust-justice, not Law-possession.
If Law-possession alone makes heirs, trust and promise are emptied.
Law exposes transgression and works wrath where violated.
Promise rests on trust so it may be grace and firm for all Abraham’s seed.
Abraham trusts the Divine who makes dead alive and calls not-being into being.
Against hope, upon hope, he trusts toward many nations.
He faces deadened body and womb without trust collapsing.
He does not waver but is empowered in trust and gives glory.
Promise is held by the power of the One who can do it.
This trust is counted as righteousness.
The counting was not written for Abraham alone.
It is also for those trusting the One who raised Jesus our Lord.
Handed over and raised, Jesus opens the field of being made righteous.

contemplative_note: Romans 4 turns Abraham into ancestor of trust across boundary. The mystical center is promise stronger than deadness: the Divine calls life where no life appears.
